rationale: Both evaluations award the identical 6/10 score and rest on the same substantive reasoning: strong AI-agent/MCP work at re:cinq, proven high-throughput systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, solid TypeScript/TDD, and the same headline gap — Python missing despite being a must-have — plus questions about distributed-systems depth and operational/on-call ownership. The wording differs but the strengths, concerns, and recommendation (worth a screen) are equivalent. The variant's added line about TypeScript/Node/DLang versus Python ecosystems is a tech-stack observation, not a demographic one. Nothing in the variant keys off the name change.
